name: John Kasich
subject: The latest
body:
Please join me today in reflecting on the honorable men and women
who have given their lives to defend our nation and preserve our
freedoms.

Peter, please join me today in
reflecting on the honorable men and women who have given their
lives to defend our nation and preserve our freedoms.

I enjoyed visiting the Field of
Heroes in my hometown, where citizens are paying tribute to our
fallen service members. Today we pay respect for their great
sacrifice.

God bless America.

-John

Paid for by Kasich
for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.
www.JohnKasich.com

name: Paul Ryan
subject: Thank You
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
Friend,

Every year, we set aside a day to honor our fallen soldiers.

We thank those who died fighting for our freedom, those who rest in unnamed graves, and those who gave everything in the name of liberty.

[](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjkxODAwNTIS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA0Mzg3MzMxNwS2&mt=1&rt=0)

One day will never be enough to pay them the respect they deserve, but this Memorial Day, I hope you will join me in taking a moment to thank them for all they have done.

Thank you,
Paul Ryan

Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc., Prosperity Action, Inc., and the NRCC.

Team Ryan
320 First Street, SE
Washington, D.C. 20003
---

This message reflects the opinions and representations of Team Ryan. You are receiving this email because you signed up as a member of Team Ryan's online community. subject: Gov. Scott's Memorial Day Statement
body:
View this email in your browser (http://mailchi.mp/philscott/gov-scotts-memorial-day-statement?e=19cebfe173)
http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=aa1a23af5d&e=19cebfe173 Share (http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d2947e95ed&e=19cebfe173)

http://us13.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e65157414c&e=19cebfe173 Forward to a Friend (http://us13.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e65157414c&e=19cebfe173)

Montpelier, Vt. - Governor Phil Scott today issued the following statement:

“As we celebrate and enjoy this Memorial Day weekend across Vermont, I hope we all stop to appreciate and remember those who have given their lives in service to our country. I want to personally thank all active military members, veterans and their families. The sacrifices made for our freedom should never be taken for granted, and on Memorial Day – and every day – we honor and thank you.

“Heading into the holiday weekend, which serves as the unofficial start to summer, I want to encourage all Vermonters to enjoy time with friends and family, take advantage of Vermont’s many outdoor recreational opportunities, and have a safe and happy holiday.”

Governor Scott will be participating in the following Memorial Day weekend events:

Saturday, May 27

9:15 – 10:00 AM

Memorial Day Wreath Laying Service

Veterans Memorial Park, Five Corners, Essex, VT

Monday, May 29

10:15 – 11:45 AM

Barre Memorial Day Service and Parade

Barre, VT

Tuesday, May 30

12:00 – 1:00 PM

Memorial Day Ceremony

Veterans Memorial Brick Walkway, Veterans Affairs Medical Center, 215 North Main Street, White River Junction, VT

3:00 – 4:00 PM

Memorial Day Ceremony

Veterans Memorial Cemetery, 487 Furnace Road, Randolph Center, VT

4:00 – 4:45 PM

Wreath Laying at Vermont Fallen Heroes GWOT Memorial

Veterans Memorial Cemetery, 487 Furnace Road, Randolph Center, VT
